items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: I'm trying to find that article you recommended last week.

options.A: Let me pull it up for you right now.
options.B: I read that article just last week.
options.C: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.D: Yes, I can recommend a good one.

items.explanation: A正确，因为'让我现在就为您调出来'是惯用的、乐于助人的回应，表示图书管理员会立即检索所需文章。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'article'和'last week'陈述个人事实。C偏离主题，转向图书馆开放时间。D是错误问题类型，回答方式像是对方在寻求推荐，而非帮助查找特定文章。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: Do you know when the tickets for the jazz concert go on sale?

options.A: I already bought mine online this morning.
options.B: I can grab one for you if you'd like.
options.C: The concert is in the student union ballroom.
options.D: Jazz music is my favorite genre.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它将语境从回答'何时'问题转向解决购票的根本问题。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'bought'和'tickets'分享无帮助的个人事实。C偏离主题，回答'何处'而非'何时'。D偏离主题，给出关于爵士乐的个人观点。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: A few of us are getting together to review for the bio final tonight.

options.A: Biology was my hardest subject last semester.
options.B: I have to review my notes first.
options.C: What time were you thinking?
options.D: The final exam is next week.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它以反问('几点？')回应，推动对话朝向接受邀请的方向发展。A偏离主题，给出关于生物学的个人观点。B是标记词引导的间接回应，可能表示拒绝而非推进对话。D是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'final'陈述相关事实。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Would it be okay if I practiced my guitar for an hour?

options.A: I don't own a guitar.
options.B: My neighbor plays the piano very loudly.
options.C: An hour is a long time to practice.
options.D: Actually, would later this evening work better?

items.explanation: D正确，因为它以话语标记词'Actually'开头，并通过提议具体替代时间间接回应请求。A偏离主题且为个人事实。B偏离主题，转向谈论其他邻居和乐器。C是错误问题类型，评论持续时间而非回应许可请求。

items.situation: responding to an invitation or suggestion
items.speaker: We're all going to try that new pizza place downtown.

options.A: Sure, I'd love to join you.
options.B: Pizza is my favorite food.
options.C: Downtown parking can be difficult.
options.D: I haven't been there yet.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它是对隐含邀请的直接、积极且自然的接受。B是语义关联陷阱，陈述关于披萨的事实。C偏离主题，转向物流问题。D偏离主题，陈述既不接受也不拒绝的个人事实。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, I'm still unclear about the formatting for the lab report.

options.A: The lab report is due on Friday.
options.B: Well, the guidelines are posted on the course website.
options.C: I formatted my report very carefully.
options.D: Yes, the lab equipment is new.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它以话语标记词'Well'开头，并通过指引学生到包含答案的资源间接回答。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'lab report'陈述截止日期。C偏离主题，为个人陈述。D是多义词/误解陷阱，将'formatting'误听为可能与实验本身相关。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: I missed class yesterday. Any chance I could take a look at your notes?

options.A: Yesterday's lecture was really interesting.
options.B: I missed the bus yesterday morning.
options.C: Just let me find them in my bag.
options.D: Yes, you should take better notes.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它以话语标记词'Just'开头，表示在满足请求前会有短暂延迟。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'yesterday'和'lecture'给出观点。B偏离主题，给出与请求无关的迟到理由。D是错误问题类型，给出建议而非回应查看笔记的请求。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Isn't the swimming pool usually open later on weekends?

options.A: I swim every weekend for exercise.
options.B: The schedule changed last month.
options.C: The pool is closed for cleaning today.
options.D: You're in luck—it's open until ten.

items.explanation: D正确，因为'You're in luck'是惯用表达，用于提供积极、相关信息，回应说话者关于当前时间的隐含问题。A偏离主题，为个人习惯。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'schedule'给出模糊、无帮助的事实。C偏离主题，回答关于'today'而非一般周末时间表。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: Do you have any change for the printer? I only have bills.

options.A: It's on me—I'll swipe my card for you.
options.B: The printer is out of paper sometimes.
options.C: I need to print my essay as well.
options.D: No, I don't have any bills either.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它将语境从回答字面'你有零钱吗'问题转向解决打印付款的根本问题。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'printer'陈述潜在问题。C偏离主题，陈述类似需求。D是错误问题类型，字面回答'没有'但误解'bills'为回答者缺少的东西。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: My desk lamp in the dorm room just stopped working.

options.A: The dorm rooms are quite spacious.
options.B: Maybe you should put in a maintenance request.
options.C: I stopped by your room earlier.
options.D: Yes, the lamp uses a special bulb.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它以话语标记词'Maybe'开头，并就如何解决问题给出有帮助的间接建议。A偏离主题，评论房间。C偏离主题，为个人陈述。D是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'lamp'给出具体但无帮助的细节。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Excuse me, do you have the book 'Introduction to Psychology'?

options.A: Yes, it's on the shelf in the social sciences section.
options.B: I'm reading a psychology book right now.
options.C: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.D: No, I haven't taken that class yet.

items.explanation: A直接回答是非问题并提供有帮助的具体位置信息。B是语义关联陷阱('psychology book')。C偏离主题(图书馆开放时间)。D回答不同类型问题(个人经历，非图书馆藏书)。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: I'm really worried about my job interview tomorrow.

options.A: I have an interview next week.
options.B: You'll do great. Just be yourself.
options.C: The job market is very competitive.
options.D: What time does it start?

items.explanation: B使用惯用鼓励语'Just be yourself'回应潜在担忧，提供 reassurance。A是语义关联陷阱('interview')。C偏离主题(一般事实)。D询问细节，这对表达关切而言是错误问题类型。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: I'm drawing a blank on what to say for the conclusion.

options.A: The conclusion is the last part of a presentation.
options.B: We should start with the introduction.
options.C: Don't sweat it. We can brainstorm some ideas.
options.D: I said we should meet at three o'clock.

items.explanation: C使用习语'Don't sweat it'提供 reassurance 并提议协作解决方案('brainstorm')，直接回应说话者问题。A是语义关联陷阱('conclusion')。B偏离主题(introduction)。D是多义词/误解陷阱('say'被理解为关于时间的过去陈述)。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I think I left my water bottle in the lecture hall.

options.A: The lecture hall is on the second floor.
options.B: I need to buy a new water bottle.
options.C: You should drink more water.
options.D: You're in luck. Someone just turned one in.

items.explanation: D使用习语'You're in luck'给出积极、相关的回应，解决说话者隐含问题。A是语义关联陷阱('lecture hall')。B偏离主题(个人需求)。C给出未经请求的建议，对丢失物品报告而言是错误回应类型。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: Do you know where I can park my car?

options.A: Are you a student or a visitor?
options.B: I parked my car over there.
options.C: Cars need regular maintenance.
options.D: No, I don't have a car.

items.explanation: A是反问，通过寻求必要信息以给出关于停车规则的有帮助答案来推进对话。B是语义关联陷阱('parked')。C偏离主题(一般事实)。D回答不同的是非问题('你有车吗？')。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: Sorry, I can't make it to the movie tonight.

options.A: The movie starts at seven.
options.B: That's okay. Maybe next time.
options.C: I didn't like the movie either.
options.D: We should get popcorn.

items.explanation: B直接恰当地接受道歉并建议未来替代方案，保持积极社交语气。A提供细节，忽略道歉。C是误解('can't make it' ≠ 不喜欢)。D偏离主题(零食计划)。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Is the swimming pool open on Sundays?

options.A: I love swimming on the weekend.
options.B: The pool is twenty-five meters long.
options.C: Actually, it's only open until noon.
options.D: No, I went there yesterday.

items.explanation: C以话语标记词'Actually'开头，提供关于周日时间的具体相关信息，间接回答是非问题。A偏离主题(个人偏好)。B是语义关联陷阱('pool'描述)。D回答不同问题('你去了吗？')。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Could you tell me how to get to the post office?

options.A: I need to mail a package.
options.B: The post office sells stamps.
options.C: I'm not from around here.
options.D: Sure, it's just two blocks down this street.

items.explanation: D以'Sure'(同意标记词)开头并给出清晰、简单的指引，直接满足请求。A陈述个人需求，未给出指引。B是语义关联陷阱('post office'功能)。C拒绝帮助，对直接请求而言是错误回应类型。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: What are you doing this Saturday afternoon?

options.A: I'm free if you need help with anything.
options.B: Saturday is my favorite day of the week.
options.C: The afternoon is usually warmer.
options.D: I did my laundry last Saturday.

items.explanation: A通过不列举活动而是提供帮助来转换语境，回应提问的潜在原因。B偏离主题(对周六的看法)。C是语义关联陷阱('afternoon'描述)。D回答关于过去的不同问题。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Do we need to include the raw data in the appendix?

options.A: The appendix comes after the references.
options.B: Well, the instructions say it's optional.
options.C: I haven't collected the data yet.
options.D: Data analysis is very important.

items.explanation: B以话语标记词'Well'开头，通过引用说明间接回答，澄清要求。A是语义关联陷阱('appendix'位置)。C偏离主题(个人状态)。D陈述一般事实，对具体程序性问题而言是错误回应类型。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you know where I can find the textbook for Professor Lee's class?

options.A: Actually, I have a copy you can borrow for the semester.
options.B: Professor Lee's class is on the third floor of this building.
options.C: I'm not sure what I'm having for lunch today.
options.D: Yes, it's a very popular textbook.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它通过提供复印本解决了根本问题（需要这本书），而不是仅仅回答字面上的'在哪里'问题。B是语义关联陷阱（'李教授的课'→课堂地点，而非书本位置）。C偏离主题且仅语法正确。D是错误的问题类型，回答了关于书本受欢迎程度的'是否'问题，而非'在哪里'问题。

items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: Will the final exam have multiple-choice questions?

options.A: I prefer multiple-choice questions to essays.
options.B: Actually, it'll be all short-answer and essay.
options.C: The exam is scheduled for next Friday morning.
options.D: No, you can't use a calculator.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它使用话语标记'Actually'礼貌地纠正假设并直接回答格式问题。A是语义关联陷阱（'多选题'→个人偏好）。C偏离主题，回答了'何时'而非'什么格式'。D是错误的问题类型，回答了不同的'是否'问题（关于计算器）。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Excuse me, how do I get to the train station from here?

options.A: I take the train to work every day.
options.B: It's a very efficient way to travel.
options.C: Go straight for two blocks, then turn left.
options.D: No, I haven't been there before.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它直接且有效地回答了'如何'问题，提供了清晰指引。A是语义关联陷阱（'火车'→个人习惯）。B偏离主题，给出了对火车的看法。D是错误的问题类型，回答了'你去过吗'的问题。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Would you mind keeping the music down a bit after ten?

options.A: I have a very large music collection.
options.B: My mind is made up about the schedule.
options.C: I finished my homework at ten o'clock.
options.D: No problem, I'll use headphones.

items.explanation: D正确，因为'No problem'是同意礼貌请求的常见惯用表达，后接具体行动。A是语义关联陷阱（'音乐'→音乐收藏）。B是多义词/误解陷阱（误解'would you mind'中的'mind'）。C偏离主题，提及时间但与请求无关。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: I'm really stressed about this presentation tomorrow.

options.A: Well, you've given great presentations before.
options.B: The presentation software is very user-friendly.
options.C: Tomorrow's weather is supposed to be sunny.
options.D: Yes, you should be stressed about it.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它以话语标记'Well'开头，提供安抚性的间接鼓励，回应感受而非演示本身。B是语义关联陷阱（'演示'→演示工具）。C偏离主题。D在语境中错误；虽然语法正确，但对表达压力的人来说是无助且不自然的回应。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: Do you have any big plans for the long weekend?

options.A: My plans are written in my calendar.
options.B: Nothing set in stone yet.
options.C: The weekend is three days long.
options.D: Yes, I made them a while ago.

items.explanation: B正确，因为'Nothing set in stone yet'是表示无明确计划的常见习语，是对问题的自然间接回答。A是语义关联陷阱（'计划'→计划记录处）。C偏离主题，陈述关于周末的事实。D是错误的问题类型，字面上回答了'是否'问题但未提供信息，未能分享任何实际计划。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I was wondering if anyone turned in a black laptop bag.

options.A: A black bag would match my coat.
options.B: I turned in my assignment this morning.
options.C: Let me check the bin for you.
options.D: No, your laptop isn't here.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它以'Let me'开头，表示将采取有帮助的行动（查看）以满足潜在需求，而非简单的是否回答。A是语义关联陷阱（'黑包'→颜色匹配）。B是多义词/误解陷阱（'turned in'意为提交作业）。D是错误的问题类型；说话者问的是包，而非笔记本电脑本身。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I just heard back from the internship I applied for.

options.A: I applied for several internships last year.
options.B: Hearing back from people is important.
options.C: The internship is located downtown.
options.D: Oh yeah? What did they say?

items.explanation: D正确，因为它使用会话短语'Oh yeah?'表达兴趣，并通过反问（'他们说了什么？'）自然推动对话。A是语义关联陷阱（'申请了'→过去的申请）。B偏离主题，做出一般性陈述。C偏离主题，提供关于实习的潜在事实。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: Are you coming to the study session at the library tonight?

options.A: I've already finished reviewing the material, but I can quiz you.
options.B: The library closes at midnight during exams.
options.C: Yes, I am coming from my dormitory.
options.D: No, I don't like studying at night.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它转换了语境；不是简单的是否回答，而是陈述了不同状态（已完成）并提供帮助，解决了共同学习的根本目标。B是语义关联陷阱（'图书馆'→图书馆开放时间）。C是直接但无助的是否回答，未推动互动。D是直接拒绝，未提供替代方案。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: Does the campus shuttle run on Sundays?

options.A: I usually run in the park on Sundays.
options.B: Yes, but it's on a reduced schedule.
options.C: The shuttle is painted blue and white.
options.D: No, I didn't take it.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它直接回答了是否问题，并提供了额外有帮助的事实信息。A是多义词/误解陷阱（'run'意为慢跑）。C是语义关联陷阱（'班车'→班车描述）。D是错误的问题类型，回答了'你坐了吗'而非'它运行吗'。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: I'm not sure if I still have the receipt for this shirt.

options.A: Don't worry, I can look up the purchase with your card.
options.B: The shirt is made from a cotton blend.
options.C: I usually keep my receipts in a folder.
options.D: Yes, the shirt is available in other colors.

items.explanation: 正确（A）解决了根本问题（无收据处理退货），而非字面上回答收据位置的不确定性。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'衬衫'→衬衫描述）。（C）偏离主题（与商店语境无关的个人习惯）。（D）是错误的问题类型（回答了关于可用性的'是否'问题，而非隐含的退货担忧）。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Isn't the project due on Friday?

options.A: Friday is my favorite day of the week.
options.B: The project is about climate change.
options.C: Actually, it was moved to next Monday.
options.D: Yes, I will do the project.

items.explanation: 正确（C）使用话语标记'Actually'礼貌地纠正并提供更新信息。（A）偏离主题（对周五的个人感受）。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'项目'→项目主题）。（D）是错误的问题类型（回答了承诺去做，而非关于截止日期的问题）。

items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: Will the final exam have multiple-choice questions?

options.A: I don't like multiple-choice tests.
options.B: The exam is in the main lecture hall.
options.C: We need to study for the final.
options.D: Why don't we ask the professor after class?

items.explanation: 正确（D）建议协作行动以获得明确答案，推动对话。（A）偏离主题（个人观点）。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'考试'→考试地点）。（C）偏离主题（关于需要学习的模糊陈述）。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Excuse me, how do I get to the city museum from here?

options.A: I'm headed there myself, I can show you the way.
options.B: The museum has a great dinosaur exhibit.
options.C: From here, you can go many places.
options.D: Yes, it's a very popular museum.

items.explanation: 正确（A）通过主动引导解决了根本需求（去博物馆），而非仅提供口头指引。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'博物馆'→展览描述）。（C）偏离主题（模糊且无助）。（D）是错误的问题类型（回答了关于受欢迎程度的'是否'问题，而非'如何到达'）。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Do we need to include graphs in the lab report?

options.A: I can create graphs on the computer.
options.B: Just the data tables are required this time.
options.C: The lab report is due next Friday.
options.D: Yes, I have included them.

items.explanation: 正确（B）使用话语标记'Just'明确要求，间接回答了'不需要图表'。（A）偏离主题（制作图表的能力，而非要求）。（C）是语义关联陷阱（'实验报告'→截止日期）。（D）是错误的问题类型（回答得好像说话者是提交者，而非关于一般要求）。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: I'm stuck on how to start our presentation.

options.A: Let's sleep on it and decide tomorrow.
options.B: The presentation is in room 205.
options.C: I'm drawing a blank too.
options.D: We started the project last week.

items.explanation: 正确（C）使用习语'drawing a blank'（想不出东西）表达共同困难，是自然的会话回应。（A）偏离主题（建议延迟，而非解决当前表达卡住的困境）。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'演示'→地点）。（D）偏离主题（提及过去行动，而非当前问题）。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: You don't happen to know if the bank's open late today, do you?

options.A: I need to deposit a check at the bank.
options.B: The bank manager is very helpful.
options.C: It closes at the usual time, 5 PM.
options.D: Your guess is as good as mine.

items.explanation: 正确（D）使用习语'Your guess is as good as mine'自然地表示不知道。（A）偏离主题（陈述说话者自身需求）。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'银行'→员工描述）。（C）是多义词/误解陷阱（将'open late'误解为询问关门时间，但给出了与可能晚营业相矛盾的明确答案）。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: When does the last campus shuttle run on weekends?

options.A: Have you tried checking the transit app?
options.B: I take the shuttle every day.
options.C: The shuttle stop is near the library.
options.D: No, it doesn't run very often.

items.explanation: 正确（A）通过建议可靠方法寻找答案来推动对话。（B）偏离主题（个人习惯，未提供时刻表）。（C）是语义关联陷阱（'班车'→站点位置）。（D）是错误的问题类型（回答了关于频率的'是否'问题，而非'何时'问题）。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I just got some pretty big news.

options.A: I'm all ears.
options.B: I heard the news report earlier.
options.C: The newspaper is on the table.
options.D: I don't like big surprises.

items.explanation: A正确，因为'I'm all ears'是习语，意为'我洗耳恭听'，是对某人表示有消息分享的自然恰当回应。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'news'但指广播报道，而非个人消息。C是语义关联陷阱，将'news'与报纸关联。D偏离主题，表达对惊喜的个人看法，这不是相关或有帮助的延续。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: What time does the campus gym open tomorrow?

options.A: I'm going to the gym later today.
options.B: At nine o'clock on Saturdays.
options.C: The gym has new exercise equipment.
options.D: No, I don't have a gym membership.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它直接且事实性地回答了关于指定日期的开放时间问题。A偏离主题，讨论说话者自己在另一天的计划。C是语义关联陷阱，提供关于健身房的事实而非其开放时间。D是错误的问题类型，回答了关于会员资格的'是否'问题，而非'什么时间'问题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, I'm not sure I understand the grading criteria for the essay.

options.A: Don't worry, I'll go over it again in class tomorrow.
options.B: The essay is due next Friday.
options.C: I think the criteria are very fair.
options.D: Yes, I understand it perfectly.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它通过承诺重新解释来转移语境，解决根本问题（困惑），而不是直接回答关于标准的问题。B是语义关联陷阱（'论文'→截止日期）。C离题（表达对公平性的看法，没有帮助）。D是错误的问题类型（回答是否理解的是非题，而非说话者表达的困惑）。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: I missed the lecture on Tuesday. Could I borrow your notes?

options.A: The lecture was in the main auditorium.
options.B: Sure, which part did you need?
options.C: I missed it too, unfortunately.
options.D: No, I don't have any notes.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它用反问来回答，通过澄清请求来推进对话。A是语义关联陷阱（'讲座'→地点）。C离题（表示说话者也错过了，没有帮助）。D是错误的问题类型（直接给出'不'，没有提供替代方案或解释）。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: Hi, are you open until 8 PM tonight?

options.A: I usually close at 6.
options.B: Tonight is a special event.
options.C: Actually, we close at 7.
options.D: Yes, I am open.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它使用标记词'实际上'礼貌地纠正假设并提供准确的关门时间。A离题（陈述个人习惯，而非营业时间）。B是语义关联陷阱（'今晚'→特别活动）。D是多义/误解陷阱（回答时仿佛说话者在询问回答者本人是否营业）。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Excuse me, is this book available for checkout?

options.A: I love reading books.
options.B: It was published last year.
options.C: The library closes in an hour.
options.D: Well, it's currently on reserve.

items.explanation: D正确，因为它使用标记词'嗯'引入间接回答，解释书籍状态（预留意味着可能无法借出）。A离题（个人观点）。B是语义关联陷阱（'书'→出版日期）。C是语义关联陷阱（'图书馆'→关门时间）。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Do you think we can finish the slides by Friday?

options.A: I've already started on them, so we should be fine.
options.B: Friday is a holiday for the university.
options.C: The slides need more images.
options.D: No, I don't think so.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它从直接回答问题转向通过提供保证和已采取的行动来解决根本担忧。B是语义关联陷阱（'周五'→假期）。C离题（批评幻灯片内容）。D是错误的问题类型（给出直接、无帮助的'不'，没有解释或替代方案）。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: I'm starving. What are you in the mood for?

options.A: I'm not very hungry right now.
options.B: I'm up for anything.
options.C: The mood in the class was serious.
options.D: We should eat at noon.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它使用惯用表达'我什么都行'来表示灵活性。A是间接拒绝（相关但没有回答'什么'的问题）。C是多义/误解陷阱（'mood'意为氛围，而非食物欲望）。D离题（建议时间，而非食物类型或地点）。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: My car's in the shop. Can you give me a lift to the airport tomorrow?

options.A: I hope your car is fixed soon.
options.B: The airport is very busy on weekends.
options.C: What time is your flight?
options.D: Maybe, but I'd need to leave by 7 AM.

items.explanation: D正确，因为它使用标记词'也许'给出有条件的间接同意，用特定限制推进对话。A离题（表达对车的希望）。B是语义关联陷阱（'机场'→一般事实）。C是反问，但询问航班时间而非解决搭车的实际问题。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Is someone available to look over my paper?

options.A: Just take a seat, and a tutor will be with you shortly.
options.B: I wrote my paper last night.
options.C: The writing center closes at 5 PM.
options.D: Yes, I am available.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它使用标记词'就'给出间接指示，满足请求（暗示会有人可用）。B离题（谈论说话者自己的论文）。C是语义关联陷阱（'写作中心'→工作时间）。D是多义/误解陷阱（回答时仿佛回答者本人可提供帮助）。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: Do you know if the pharmacy is open on Sundays?

options.A: Yes, it's open from 10 AM to 4 PM.
options.B: I need to pick up a prescription too.
options.C: The pharmacy is right next to the bank.
options.D: No, I haven't been there yet.

items.explanation: A直接回答关于周日时间的实际问题。B是语义关联陷阱（'药房'→'处方'）。C是语义关联陷阱（'药房'→位置）。D回答不同问题（'你去过那里吗'）。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: I'm starving. Where should we go for lunch?

options.A: I'm not hungry yet.
options.B: I've got a coupon for the new sandwich place.
options.C: The cafeteria closes at two.
options.D: I usually eat a big breakfast.

items.explanation: B通过提出有帮助的建议来解决根本问题（决定去哪里），转移了语境。A是间接拒绝但没有推进决定。C是语义关联陷阱（'午餐'→'食堂'时间）。D离题（个人习惯）。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Does the lab report need to be double-spaced?

options.A: I haven't started my report yet.
options.B: The lab was really interesting this week.
options.C: Yes, and use 12-point font.
options.D: No, you don't have to do that.

items.explanation: C直接回答是非题并添加相关细节。A离题（个人状态）。B是语义关联陷阱（'实验室'→对实验室的评论）。D是错误的问题类型（回答'必须'的问题，而非'需要'的问题）。

items.situation: student asking about course enrollment or prerequisites
items.speaker: Do I need to take Calculus I before signing up for Physics?

options.A: Calculus is a challenging subject.
options.B: The physics lab is in the science building.
options.C: I signed up for it last semester.
options.D: Have you checked the course catalog online?

items.explanation: D通过建议查找官方答案的地方（反问）来推进对话。A是语义关联陷阱（'微积分'→难度评论）。B是语义关联陷阱（'物理'→位置）。C离题（个人过去行为）。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: Where does the campus shuttle pick up near the library?

options.A: Are you heading to the student union or the dorms?
options.B: I always walk to the library.
options.C: The library has extended hours during finals.
options.D: It's a very reliable service.

items.explanation: A是反问，寻求澄清以给出更有帮助的回答。B离题（个人习惯）。C是语义关联陷阱（'图书馆'→时间）。D是语义关联陷阱（'班车'→一般评论）。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: Are you doing anything fun this weekend?

options.A: The weekend is my favorite part of the week.
options.B: Actually, I might go hiking if the weather's nice.
options.C: I have a lot of homework to finish.
options.D: No, I didn't realize that.

items.explanation: B使用话语标记'实际上'给出关于潜在计划的间接但相关的回答。A离题（一般评论）。C是间接拒绝但没有涉及'有趣'的计划。D是错误的问题类型（回答'意识到'的问题）。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Sorry, I'm a bit lost. How do I get to the train station from here?

options.A: I don't take the train very often.
options.B: You should leave soon to avoid traffic.
options.C: You're in luck, I'm headed that way myself.
options.D: The station was renovated last year.

items.explanation: C使用惯用表达'你运气真好'表示说话者可以通过引导来帮助。A离题（个人频率）。B是语义关联陷阱（'火车站'→旅行建议）。D是语义关联陷阱（'车站'→描述）。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I was wondering if anyone turned in a black laptop bag.

options.A: I need a new laptop bag too.
options.B: The front desk closes at five o'clock.
options.C: My laptop is working perfectly.
options.D: Let me take a look for you.

items.explanation: D使用有帮助的惯用提议'让我帮你看看'，显示合作回应。A是语义关联陷阱（'笔记本电脑包'→个人需求）。B离题（时间）。C是语义关联/多义陷阱（'笔记本电脑'→设备状态）。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: When's the absolute latest we can submit the project?

options.A: Don't stress, I've already uploaded our final draft.
options.B: The project guidelines are on the course website.
options.C: Midnight, I think.
options.D: I submitted mine early last time.

items.explanation: A通过表明任务已完成来解决对截止日期的根本焦虑，转移了语境。B是语义关联陷阱（'项目'→指南）。C直接回答字面问题但语用帮助较小。D离题（个人过去行为）。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Would it bother you if I practiced the piano for an hour tonight?

options.A: I don't know how to play the piano.
options.B: Actually, I'll be out at a concert until late.
options.C: The piano is a beautiful instrument.
options.D: Yes, it would be very loud.

items.explanation: B通过提供信息（外出）间接使原问题无关，暗示'请便'，从而转移语境。A离题（个人技能）。C是语义关联陷阱（'钢琴'→评论）。D直接回答'是'但生硬且不合作。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the train station?

options.A: Actually, it's just a couple of blocks straight ahead.
options.B: I'm going to the station later today.
options.C: The train schedule is posted online.
options.D: No, I don't have a train ticket.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它使用话语标记'实际上'礼貌地给出请求的指引。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'车站'谈论说话者自己的计划。C离题，提供时间表信息而非指引。D是错误的问题类型，回答关于拥有的是非题而非'如何到达'的问题。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: Hi, I'd like to return this shirt I bought last week.

options.A: I bought a similar shirt last month.
options.B: Sure, do you have the receipt with you?
options.C: The shirt is made from cotton and polyester.
options.D: No, you can't wear it outside.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它使用'当然'作为标记词来确认请求，并通过相关问题推进流程。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'买'和'衬衫'讲述个人轶事。C离题，描述材料而非处理退货。D是错误的问题类型，对意图陈述给出禁止性'不'的回答。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: I was wondering if the pharmacy is still open.

options.A: The pharmacy sells many different products.
options.B: I'm not sure, let me check the website for you.
options.C: You're in luck, we close in thirty minutes.
options.D: No, I haven't been to the pharmacy.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它使用惯用表达'You're in luck'来传达积极、间接的回答，表明药店仍在营业。A是语义关联陷阱，描述药店本身而非营业时间。B是标记引导的间接回应，但表达了不确定性并建议核实，不如惯用确认直接和有用。D是错误问题类型，回答得像是被问及个人经历。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: Is the bank open on Saturdays?

options.A: I need to deposit a check at the bank.
options.B: The bank manager is very helpful.
options.C: Saturday is usually my day off.
options.D: As a matter of fact, it opens at ten.

items.explanation: D正确，因为它使用惯用短语'As a matter of fact'来引入对营业时间的直接且有用的确认。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'bank'来陈述个人需求。B是另一个语义关联陷阱，描述银行员工。C偏离主题，讨论回答者的个人日程。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, when are you holding your extra review session?

options.A: The session will cover chapters four and five.
options.B: Don't worry, I've posted all the details online.
options.C: I reviewed the material last night.
options.D: Yes, I am holding it tomorrow.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它进行了语境转换，不回答'何时'的问题，而是通过说明信息已可用来解决潜在需求。A是语义关联陷阱，提供内容细节而非时间。C偏离主题，讨论个人学习习惯。D是直接的主题回答，对于难题来说较不常见，是更简单、字面的回应。

items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: Will the final exam include any essay questions?

options.A: I find essay questions quite challenging.
options.B: The final is scheduled for next Friday.
options.C: Actually, it'll be multiple choice only.
options.D: No, you can't use a dictionary.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它使用话语标记'Actually'来给出关于格式的清晰、间接回答。A是语义关联陷阱，给出对论文的个人看法。B偏离主题，提供考试日期而非格式。D是错误问题类型，对关于规则的不同隐含问题给出禁止性的'No'。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: I missed the lecture on Tuesday. Could I borrow your notes?

options.A: Tuesday's weather was really nice.
options.B: I take notes on my laptop.
options.C: The lecture was in the main auditorium.
options.D: Sure, just let me find them for you.

items.explanation: D正确，因为它使用标记'Sure'和'just'来表示同意并指示一个简短的过程。A是语义关联陷阱，抓住'Tuesday'来评论天气。B是另一个语义关联陷阱，描述记笔记的方法而非回应请求。C偏离主题，提供讲座地点。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Is someone available to help me with my paper?

options.A: Yes, the next tutor is free in about five minutes.
options.B: I wrote my paper last weekend.
options.C: The writing center has new computers.
options.D: No, I don't have a paper to write.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它直接且有效地回答了关于可用性的是非问题。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'paper'来陈述个人声明。C偏离主题，描述中心的设施。D是错误问题类型，回答得像是被问及个人义务。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: A bunch of us are thinking of catching a movie later.

options.A: I saw a great movie last week.
options.B: Count me in, that sounds fun.
options.C: The movie theater is downtown.
options.D: No, I'm not thinking about it.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它使用惯用表达'Count me in'来表达对隐含邀请的热烈同意加入。A是语义关联陷阱，讨论过去的电影经历。C偏离主题，陈述剧院的位置。D是多义/误解陷阱，从字面上理解'thinking'并给出矛盾的'No'回答。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: I'm not sure how to start my personal statement.

options.A: What's the main point you're trying to get across?
options.B: The writing center is on the second floor.
options.C: I started my essay last night.
options.D: Yes, you should definitely write one.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它通过提出一个有助于澄清说话者潜在需求的问题来推进对话。B是语义关联陷阱（来自隐含情境的'writing center'）。C偏离主题，转向回答者自己的论文。D是错误问题类型，将陈述视为关于必要性的是非问题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, I was hoping to discuss my paper draft today.

options.A: I hope you have a good discussion.
options.B: Actually, my office hours are tomorrow from two to four.
options.C: The paper draft is due next week.
options.D: I discussed it with my friend yesterday.

items.explanation: B正确；'Actually'表示温和的纠正或转换，提供必要信息（教授何时有空）而非直接的是非回答。A偏离主题且无帮助。C是语义关联陷阱（'paper draft' → 截止日期）。D是多义/误解陷阱，在不同语境中使用'discussed'。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: Is the pharmacy still open?

options.A: I need to pick up a prescription.
options.B: The pharmacy sells over-the-counter medicine.
options.C: Just until seven tonight.
options.D: Yes, I opened it this morning.

items.explanation: C正确；'Just'缓和了回应并提供了具体的关门时间，这是有用的间接回答。A偏离主题（陈述需求）。B是语义关联陷阱（'pharmacy' → 它销售什么）。D是多义/误解陷阱（'open'作为解锁/开始 vs. 营业）。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: Do you want to join our study session at the library later?

options.A: The library has extended hours during finals.
options.B: I prefer to study alone.
options.C: Yes, I joined a study group last semester.
options.D: I've actually already finished reviewing that chapter.

items.explanation: D正确；它通过提供一个理由（工作已完成）来转换语境，间接处理了邀请的潜在目的，使得直接的是非回答变得不必要。A是语义关联陷阱（'library' → 营业时间）。B偏离主题（陈述一般偏好）。C是错误问题类型，回答'yes'但带有不相关的过去信息。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I just got some pretty exciting news.

options.A: Don't keep me in suspense!
options.B: The news today was mostly about politics.
options.C: I didn't get any news today.
options.D: Yes, it is very exciting.

items.explanation: A正确；它使用成语'Don't keep me in suspense!'来表达热切的兴趣，这是一种自然且投入的回应。B是语义关联陷阱（'news' → 当前事件）。C偏离主题（转向回答者）。D是错误问题类型，将陈述视为关于兴奋程度的是非问题。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: I'm completely swamped with this move to my new apartment.

options.A: The new apartment must be nice.
options.B: Tell you what, I can help you pack some boxes on Saturday.
options.C: I moved to a new city last year.
options.D: Are you swamped with work or school?

items.explanation: B正确；它通过提供实际帮助（'Tell you what'表示提议）来转换语境，以解决潜在问题而非评论情境。A偏离主题（做出假设）。C偏离主题（分享个人轶事）。D是错误问题类型，询问被淹没的来源，这帮助较小。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: How much does it cost to print a page here?

options.A: I printed my essay yesterday.
options.B: The computer lab closes at ten.
options.C: Ten cents per page for black and white.
options.D: Yes, you can print as many as you need.

items.explanation: C正确；它直接且事实性地回答了'how much'问题。A偏离主题（个人过去行为）。B是语义关联陷阱（'computer lab' → 营业时间）。D是错误问题类型，回答关于许可而非费用的是非问题。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I'm looking for a black backpack I might have left in the auditorium.

options.A: The auditorium seats five hundred people.
options.B: I left my backpack at home today.
options.C: A black backpack is very common.
options.D: Can you describe any distinctive features?

items.explanation: D正确；它通过提出一个实际的、澄清性的问题来推进对话，以帮助定位特定物品。A是语义关联陷阱（'auditorium' → 容量）。B偏离主题（回答者自身情况）。C偏离主题（陈述明显事实，无帮助）。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: Isn't the bank open on Saturdays?

options.A: Well, only the main branch is, from nine to noon.
options.B: I need to deposit a check at the bank.
options.C: The bank manager is very helpful.
options.D: No, I don't bank there.

items.explanation: A正确；'Well'表示有条件的回答，提供具体的、有用的信息而非简单的是非回答。B偏离主题（陈述需求）。C是语义关联陷阱（'bank' → 描述员工）。D是错误问题类型，回答个人的'do you'问题。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: I need to reschedule my dentist appointment for next week.

options.A: My dentist appointment is tomorrow.
options.B: Let me check the calendar and see what's available.
options.C: The dentist's office is on Maple Street.
options.D: Yes, you should go to the dentist regularly.

items.explanation: B正确；它将语境转向行动（检查可用性）以解决潜在问题（寻找新时间）而非评论陈述。A偏离主题（回答者自己的预约）。C是语义关联陷阱（'dentist' → 位置）。D是错误问题类型，给出建议而非处理重新安排请求。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Is it too late to run the vacuum cleaner?

options.A: Don't worry about it. I'm wearing headphones.
options.B: The vacuum is in the storage closet.
options.C: I cleaned the whole apartment yesterday.
options.D: No, it's not too heavy to carry.

items.explanation: 正确（A）通过表明不会造成问题来解决关于打扰邻居的潜在担忧，而非回答字面的时间问题。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'vacuum' → 位置）。（C）偏离主题（谈论清洁，而非噪音/时间）。（D）是错误问题类型/多义陷阱（将'late'解释为'heavy'或回答不同问题）。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: I missed the lecture on market trends. Can I see your notes?

options.A: The market trends were very predictable.
options.B: Be my guest. They're right here.
options.C: I missed the bus this morning.
options.D: No, you can't see them from there.

items.explanation: 正确（B）使用成语'Be my guest'以友好的方式授予许可。（A）是语义关联陷阱（'market trends' → 评论主题）。（C）偏离主题（在不同语境中重复使用'missed'）。（D）是多义/误解陷阱（从字面上理解'see'为视觉能力）。

items.situation: coordinating airport pickup with a friend
items.speaker: Do you remember which airline she's flying with?

options.A: I always fly with that airline.
options.B: The airport is about thirty minutes away.
options.C: I've got her flight info right here.
options.D: Yes, I remember meeting her last year.

items.explanation: 正确（C）通过提供解决方案（拥有信息）来转换语境，而非回答记忆问题。（A）是语义关联陷阱（'airline' → 个人习惯）。（B）是语义关联陷阱（'airport' → 位置/距离）。（D）是错误问题类型（回答'Do you remember...'但关于不同事件）。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: You don't happen to know if the bank's still open, do you?

options.A: The bank teller was very helpful.
options.B: I need to deposit a check soon.
options.C: Yes, I opened an account there.
options.D: As a matter of fact, it closes at five.

items.explanation: 正确选项(D)使用会话短语'实际上'来引出有帮助的事实性回答。(A)是语义关联陷阱('银行'→员工描述)。(B)偏离主题(说话者自身需求，而非信息)。(C)是错误问题类型/多义性陷阱(将'开立'误解为开设账户)。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Have you finished your part of the project yet?

options.A: Actually, I'm putting the final touches on it now.
options.B: The project is due on Friday.
options.C: I finished my homework earlier.
options.D: Yes, I have a part-time job.

items.explanation: 正确选项(A)使用'其实'作为标记，间接表明接近完成。(B)是语义关联陷阱('项目'→截止日期事实)。(C)偏离主题(转向'作业')。(D)偏离主题(无关个人细节)。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I was wondering if anyone turned in a black laptop bag.

options.A: I turned in my assignment yesterday.
options.B: Let me check the log for you.
options.C: My laptop has a black case.
options.D: No, I haven't wondered about that.

items.explanation: 正确选项(B)使用'让我...'来回应查询，表示提供帮助的行动。(A)是多义性/误解陷阱('上交'理解为提交作业)。(C)是语义关联陷阱('电脑包'→个人物品描述)。(D)是错误问题类型(字面回答'想知道')。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Do you know when the fitness center opens on weekdays?

options.A: I need to get more fit.
options.B: The center of town is quite far.
options.C: It opens at six in the morning.
options.D: Yes, I know several people there.

items.explanation: 正确选项(C)直接且事实性地回答了关于开放时间的问题。(A)偏离主题(个人意图)。(B)是多义性陷阱('中心'被误解)。(D)是错误问题类型(回答'你知道吗'但未提供所需信息)。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: My dorm room window won't stay shut. Who should I contact?

options.A: The window has a nice view.
options.B: You should shut it quietly.
options.C: I contacted my friend earlier.
options.D: Just submit a work order online.

items.explanation: 正确选项(D)使用'只需'标记一个简单、间接的解决方案(流程)而非指定人员。(A)是语义关联陷阱('窗户'→描述)。(B)是误解(将'关闭'视为动作指令)。(C)偏离主题(在不同语境中重复使用'联系')。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: I'm completely swamped with this move to the new apartment.

options.A: Need a hand? I'm free this afternoon.
options.B: The new apartment is much bigger.
options.C: I moved last year as well.
options.D: No, you didn't swamp it.

items.explanation: 正确选项(A)使用习语'需要帮忙吗？'恰当地提供帮助。(B)是语义关联陷阱('公寓'→描述)。(C)偏离主题(分享过去经历，而非帮助)。(D)是多义性/误解陷阱(字面理解'忙得不可开交')。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Can you give me a ride to the train station tomorrow?

options.A: I gave him a ride last week.
options.B: Sure, what time do you need to be there?
options.C: The train station is being renovated.
options.D: Yes, I can ride a bicycle.

items.explanation: 正确选项(B)直接同意并询问必要的后续信息。(A)偏离主题(与不同人的过去事件)。(C)是语义关联陷阱('火车站'→相关事实)。(D)是多义性/误解陷阱('乘车'理解为驾驶车辆而非乘坐)。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Would it bother you if I played some music for a while?

options.A: I love listening to music.
options.B: How loud are you planning to have it?
options.C: My headphones are broken.
options.D: No, I haven't heard it yet.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它以反问('声音多大...？')回应，寻求澄清以解决对噪音水平的潜在担忧，推动对话。A是语义关联陷阱('音乐'→'喜欢听')但未回答许可请求。C偏离主题，讨论个人物品。D是错误问题类型，回答'你听过吗'而非处理未来请求。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: I've got a conflict and can't make the meeting on Thursday.

options.A: Thursday is usually a busy day.
options.B: The meeting is in the conference room.
options.C: What day would work better for you?
options.D: No, I didn't schedule it.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它使用反问('周几...？')来提议寻找替代方案，这是对时间冲突自然且有帮助的回应。A是语义关联陷阱('周四'→'忙碌的一天')但无帮助。B偏离主题，提供地点细节。D是错误问题类型，回答一个未被问及的'你安排了吗'问题。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Excuse me, is this book available for checkout?

options.A: I need to check out some books too.
options.B: The author is very famous.
options.C: It's on the shelf over there.
options.D: Yes, you can borrow it for three weeks.

items.explanation: D正确，因为它直接且事实性地回答了关于可用性的是非问句，并提供相关附加信息(借阅期限)，符合'简单'直接主题范式。A偏离主题，转向说话者自身需求。B是语义关联陷阱('书'→'作者')。C是多义性/误解陷阱；'借出'意为借阅，但此回应误解为'找到'。

items.situation: responding to an invitation or suggestion
items.speaker: Do you want to go see that new action movie tonight?

options.A: I've actually already seen it, but we could grab dinner instead.
options.B: Action movies are my favorite genre.
options.C: Tonight, I have to finish a report.
options.D: No, I don't like going out at night.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它使用了语境转换；未对电影简单回答是/否，而是陈述理由(已看过)并转向解决潜在社交目标，提议替代方案(晚餐)。B是语义关联陷阱('动作片'→'最喜欢的类型')但未回应邀请。C偏离主题，讨论个人任务。D是错误问题类型；虽拒绝，但其理由('不喜欢晚上外出')是宽泛的拒绝，不符合'困难'项目的具体语用语境。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: When does the guest lecture by the scientist start?

options.A: Scientists often give fascinating talks.
options.B: Don't worry about the time, I saved us seats near the front.
options.C: The lecture hall is in the science building.
options.D: Yes, it's open to all students.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它执行了语境转换；未回答'何时'问题，而是通过陈述问题已解决('帮我们留了座位')来处理潜在担忧(占好位置)。A是语义关联陷阱('科学家'→'精彩演讲')。C偏离主题，提供地点而非时间。D是错误问题类型，回答'开放吗'问题。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: I'm kind of in the mood for a sandwich.

options.A: Sandwiches are usually quick to make.
options.B: I need to eat lunch soon.
options.C: Well, there's that great deli on Maple Street.
options.D: No, I had a big breakfast.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它以话语标记'嗯'开头，并通过建议一个供应三明治的具体地点间接同意，符合'简单'项目的标记引导间接范式。A是语义关联陷阱('三明治'→'制作快捷')。B偏离主题，陈述个人需求。D是错误问题类型，对非是非问句的陈述给出'不'的回应。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: You don't happen to know if the bank's still open, do you?

options.A: Banks handle important transactions.
options.B: I need to deposit a check.
options.C: It closed an hour ago, I'm afraid.
options.D: Your best bet is to call them.

items.explanation: D正确，因为'你最好打电话问问'是习语表达，在说话者没有信息时建议最可靠的做法，这是自然、有帮助的回应。A是语义关联陷阱('银行'→'处理交易')。B偏离主题，陈述说话者自身差事。C是多义性/误解陷阱；它字面回答问题，但对于'中等'项目及习语范式，正确答案应是间接的，此直接回答是干扰项。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: My parents are coming to visit this weekend.

options.A: Say no more, I'll make sure the place is tidy.
options.B: Parents often enjoy visiting campuses.
options.C: I'm going home this weekend too.
options.D: No, they arrived yesterday.

items.explanation: A正确，因为'不用说了，我会确保地方整洁'是习语表达，显示立即理解并根据隐含需求提供帮助行动(打扫)。B是语义关联陷阱('父母'→'喜欢来访')。C偏离主题，讨论听者自身计划。D是错误问题类型，以'不'和错误时间回答'他们要来吗'问题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, I was wondering if the deadline for the essay is flexible.

options.A: Essays require a lot of research.
options.B: Actually, I can give you an extra day if you need it.
options.C: I have to finish my essay tonight.
options.D: Yes, you wrote it very well.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它以话语标记'其实'开头，并通过提供延期间接同意，符合'简单'项目的标记引导间接范式。A是语义关联陷阱('论文'→'需要研究')。C偏离主题，陈述听者自身任务。D是多义性/误解陷阱；'想知道截止日期是否灵活'被误解为询问已完成论文的质量。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: Do you know which chapters we're supposed to read for next week?

options.A: I haven't read the chapters yet either.
options.B: Have you checked the syllabus online?
options.C: The library has a copy of the textbook.
options.D: Next week's lecture is on Tuesday.

items.explanation: 正确选项(B)通过建议实用解决方案(查看教学大纲)而非直接回答来推动对话。(A)偏离主题(分享未采取行动但无帮助)。(C)是语义关联陷阱('阅读'/'章节'→'教科书'/'图书馆')。(D)是语义关联陷阱('下周'→讲座日，忽略阅读问题)。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Can you give me a ride to the train station tomorrow?

options.A: The train station is quite far from here.
options.B: Sure, what time do you need to be there?
options.C: I usually take the bus to work.
options.D: No, I didn't take the train.

items.explanation: 正确选项(B)直接且有帮助地同意并询问必要的后续信息。(A)偏离主题(评论距离，未处理请求)。(C)偏离主题(个人习惯)。(D)是多义性/误解陷阱(误解'搭车'为'乘火车')。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you know if this journal is available online?

options.A: The journal was published last month.
options.B: I read it online yesterday.
options.C: Would you like me to check the database?
options.D: No, I'm not available right now.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它以有帮助的反问回应，通过提议查找所请求的具体信息来推动对话。A是语义关联陷阱，提供期刊出版事实而非其可用性。B偏离主题，陈述个人过去行为，对询问无帮助。D是多义性/误解陷阱，误解'可用'为指图书管理员的时间而非期刊的可获取性。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: Where's the best place to park for the science building?

options.A: The science building has a new planetarium.
options.B: Parking is always a problem here.
options.C: You can park in my spot today, I'm not using it.
options.D: It's on the north side of campus.

items.explanation: C正确，因为它转换语境，通过提供直接解决方案来解决潜在问题(找停车位)，而非描述地点。A是语义关联陷阱('科学楼'→设施)。B偏离主题(对停车的普遍抱怨)。D是错误问题类型(描述建筑位置，而非停车地点)。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: Do you know when the next shuttle to the library leaves?

options.A: I usually walk to the library.
options.B: Actually, I think one just left a minute ago.
options.C: The library has extended hours during finals.
options.D: Yes, I know the schedule.

items.explanation: B正确，因为它使用'Actually'这一标记词，间接但有效地提供了班车最近已出发的信息，暗示下一班需要等待。A偏离主题（个人习惯）。C是语义关联陷阱（'图书馆'→开放时间）。D是错误的问题类型（回答了字面'你知道吗'的问题，未提供所需信息）。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: Do you know if this scanner is working?

options.A: How about trying the one by the window?
options.B: I scanned my notes yesterday.
options.C: The computer lab closes at midnight.
options.D: Yes, I know how to use it.

items.explanation: A正确，因为它以反问形式回应，提出了实用的替代解决方案。B是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'scanned'描述过去的个人行为。C偏离主题，讨论实验室开放时间而非扫描仪功能。D是多义词/误解陷阱，将'know if'理解为'know how to'。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Do you know if the gym is still open this late?

options.A: I'm not sure, but I can check for you.
options.B: The gym has new treadmills.
options.C: I'm going to be late for my class.
options.D: Yes, I was there yesterday.

items.explanation: A正确，因为'我不确定，但可以帮你查一下'是地道、有帮助的回应，主动提出查找信息，适合处理'困难'项目。B是语义关联陷阱，将'健身房'与'跑步机'关联但未解答开放时间。C偏离主题，讨论个人日程。D是错误的问题类型，回答'你当时在吗'而非'现在是否开放'。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure if we need to include the raw data in the lab report.

options.A: Actually, the syllabus says just the summary tables are required.
options.B: The data was collected in the chemistry lab.
options.C: I'm not sure about the weather forecast for tomorrow.
options.D: Yes, you should include all the data you have.

items.explanation: A正确。它以话语标记词'Actually'开头，提供关于所需条件的相关信息，解决了根本的不确定性。B是语义关联陷阱，提及'数据'和'实验室'但陈述了无关的收集地点信息。C偏离主题，完全转向不同话题（天气）。D是错误的问题类型；给出直接、无条件的'是'回答，与正确、细致的回应矛盾，不符合助教的典型建议方式。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Excuse me, are you available to look over my essay draft?

options.A: I wrote my essay last night.
options.B: Yes, I have about twenty minutes before my next appointment.
options.C: The draft is on the desk over there.
options.D: No, I don't have a pen.

items.explanation: B正确。它直接、有帮助地回答了关于可用性的是否问题，并提供了相关、有用的时间信息。A是语义关联陷阱，重复使用'essay'分享无关的个人事实。C是语义关联陷阱，关注'draft'的位置而非可用性。D偏离主题，回答了未提及的不同需求（书写工具）。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: I'd like to return this sweater, but I lost the receipt.

options.A: The sweater is made of wool.
options.B: I bought a sweater last week.
options.C: Well, I can offer you store credit if you have the card you used.
options.D: Yes, you lost it yesterday.

items.explanation: C正确。它以'Well,'开头，表示对问题（无收据退货）提出有条件或间接的解决方案，提供了潜在替代方案（凭卡获得商店积分）。A是语义关联陷阱，描述毛衣材质，与退货流程无关。B偏离主题，陈述无关的个人购买行为。D是多义词/误解陷阱，字面上确认收据丢失，仿佛在回答是否问题，这对推进对话无帮助。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Hi, I was wondering if anyone turned in a black laptop bag.

options.A: My laptop is black.
options.B: The bag is under the table.
options.C: I turned in my assignment this morning.
options.D: Yes, we have one that came in this afternoon.

items.explanation: D正确。它直接、信息充分地回答了关于失物招领处是否有包的是否问题。A是语义关联陷阱，关注'笔记本电脑'颜色而非包。B偏离主题，给出位置指引仿佛已知包的存在，未回答查询。C是语义关联陷阱，在学术作业而非失物情境中误用'turned in'。
